mechanism_narrative: A sustained rising trend in industrial production signals expanding aggregate output, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO trailing-6m change on 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to separate positive and negative return periods beyond random chance (binomial test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it indicates labor market slack that suppresses consumer demand and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances triggered by UNRATE > 4.5%, or the mean return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A negative real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS below trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation) signals accommodative monetary policy that compresses discount rates and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return during negative real rate periods is not significantly greater than baseline mean (t-test p>0.05), or the positive signal fails in more than 40% of instances (i.e., negative returns).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, declining housing starts signal weakening demand that amplifies the negative impact of tighter policy on the economy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In periods outside clear tightening cycles, housing starts declines produce positive or neutral trend-aligned returns; or the negative signal in tightening cycles fails to produce a significantly negative mean return (p>0.05) compared to non-tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1% signals a sudden drop in consumer spending that contracts aggregate demand, foreshadowing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices earnings risk.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average magnitude of negative returns is less than -0.5% and not statistically different from zero (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth stalls (monthly change below zero) while the unemployment rate remains elevated above 4.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that undermines consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), or the joint signal fails to add predictive value over either individual series alone (p-value of interaction term >0.10 in linear regression).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP is the broadest measure of economic output; a sustained upward trend in GDPC1 reflects robust aggregate demand and rising corporate profits, which supports higher equity prices over the medium term (one quarter).
failure_condition: The trailing four-quarter change in GDPC1 fails to exhibit a significant positive rank correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal is dominated by inflation-adjusted variables.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle economic regime, rising CPI inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, compressing valuations and reducing equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In non-late-cycle regimes, rising CPI does not lead to negative returns; or the negative signal in late-cycle fails to produce a mean return significantly below the overall sample mean (p>0.05) in a two-sample t-test.
